points.name: Volt-Var Enable Autonomous Voltage Reference Adjustment
System Communication Error
points.state_1: Alarm: Communications error exists in the ESS

points.name: System Has Priority 1 Alarms
points.state_1: Alarm: One or More P1 Alarms Active

points.name: DER is Off but Available to Start
DER is Off and Not Available to Start
Operating Mode - Active Power Limit Enabled
Operating Mode - Constant Power Factor Enabled
Low/High Voltage Ride-Through High Must Trip Curve Index.  Index of the Voltage Ride-through curve which specifies trip points when the voltage is high. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.
Low/High Voltage Ride-Through Low Must Trip Curve Index.  Index of the Voltage Ride-through curve which specifies trip points when the voltage is low. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.

Low/High Voltage Ride-Through High Momentary Cessation Curve Index.  Index of the Voltage Ride-through curve which specifies where generation/discharging must stop when the voltage is high. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.

Low/High Voltage Ride-Through Low Momentary Cessation Curve Index.  Index of the Voltage Ride-through curve which specifies where generation/discharging must stop when the voltage is low. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.

Low/High Frequency Ride-Through High Must Trip Curve Index.  Index of the Frequency Ride-through curve which specifies trip points when the frequency is high. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.

Low/High Frequency Ride-Through Low Must Trip Curve Index.  Index of the Frequency Ride-through curve which specifies trip points when the frequency is low. A zero (0) values means the curve is not used.
Frequency-Watt High Discharging / Generating Gradient
points.units: Percent watts per percent Hz difference

points.name: Frequency-Watt Low Discharging / Generating  Gradient
points.units: Percent watts per percent Hz difference

points.name: Active Power Limit Maximum (Pos = Generation, Neg = Consumption)
Active Power Limit Minimum (Pos = Generation, Neg = Consumption)
Volt-Var Autonomous Voltage Reference Adjustment Time Constant
Nameplate Active Generation Power Rating at Unity Power Factor
Nameplate Active Generation Power Rating at Specified Over-Excited Power Factor, injecting reactive power (as positive vars)
Nameplate Active Generation Power Rating at Specified Under-Excited Power Factor, absorbing reactive power (as negative vars)
Nameplate Active Charging Power Rating at Specified Under-Excited Power Factor
Nameplate Storage Actual Energy Capacity. Nameplate (original) actual total energy capacity of the storage system expressed in Storage Capacity Units.

IEEE 1547 Normal Operating Performance Category.  Enumeration:
<0> unknown
<1> Category A
<2> Category B

IEEE 1547 Abnormal Operating Performance Category.  Enumeration:
<0> unknown
<1> Category I
<2> Category II
<3> Category III
System Usable State of Charge.  Present usable energy in the DER as a percentage of Nameplate Storage Usable Capacity.
Frequency-Watt High Starting Frequency.  Delta frequency between start frequency and nominal grid frequency for High frequency events.
Frequency-Watt High Discharging / Generating Gradient
points.units: Percent watts per percent Hz difference

points.name: Frequency-Watt Low Starting Frequency.  Delta frequency between start frequency and nominal grid frequency for low frequency events.
Frequency-Watt Low Discharging / Generating Gradient
points.units: Percent watts per percent Hz difference

points.name: Frequency-Watt Ramp Up Time Constant. Time constant or open loop response time for moving from the current active power target to a Higher active power target.
Frequency-Watt Ramp Down Time Constant. Time constant or open loop response time for moving from the current active power target to a lower  active power target.
Active Power Limit Maximum (Pos = Generation, Neg = Consumption)
Active Power Limit Minimum (Pos = Generation, Neg = Consumption)
Volt-Watt Reference Voltage Input.  Voltage measurement read from the meter and used as an input to the mode.
Volt-Watt Curve Index.  Index of the Volt-Watt curve that should be used by the mode. If zero, then direct paramters are used.
Constant VArs Reactive Power Target.  Percentage of maximum reactive power.
Constant Power Factor Setpoint - Generation / Discharging

System Meter Type of Connection Point.   Enumeration:
<0> unknown,
<1> DER to local EPS
<2>  Internal to DER
<3> local EPS with load to area EPS (PCC with load)
<4> local EPS w/o load to area EPS (PCC without load)
<5> Load to local EPS
<6> External to DER beyond the PCC
<7>  External to DER within the local EPS
<8> Auxiliary DER Load
<9> Group of DERs to the area EPS
<99> Other

System Meter Type of Circuit Phases.   Enumeration:
<0> unknown
<1> Single phase
<2> Split phase
<3> 2-phase
<4> 3-phase delta
<5> 3-phase wye
<6> 3-phase wye grounded
<7> 3-phase / 3-wire (inverter type)
<8> 3-phase / 4-wire (inverter type)

System Meter Apparent Power Calculation Method.  Calculation method for total apparent power calculation.  Enumeration:
     <0> unknown
     <1> vector
     <2> aritMeteretic
System Meter Average Line to Line Voltage
System Meter Active Power  – High Threshold
System Meter Active Power  – Low Threshold
System Meter Reactive Power  – High Threshold
System Meter Reactive Power  – Low Threshold
System Meter Power Factor  – High Threshold
System Meter Power Factor  – Low Threshold
System Meter Phase A Volts – High Threshold
System Meter Phase A Volts – Low Threshold
System Meter Phase B Volts – High Threshold
System Meter Phase B Volts – Low Threshold
System Meter Phase C Volts – High Threshold
System Meter Phase C Volts – Low Threshold
Frequency-Watt Droop High Discharging / Generating Gradient
Frequency-Watt Droop Low Discharging / Generating Gradient
High Frequency-Watt Droop Minimum Active Power
Low Frequency-Watt Droop Maximum Active Power
Active Power Limit Maximum (Pos = Generation, Neg = Consumption)
Active Power Limit Minimum (Pos = Generation, Neg = Consumption)
Constant Vars using vars as unit

curve_type.name:
Curve Type.  Enumeration:

 <0> Curve disabled
 <1>  Not applicable / Unknown
 <2>  Volt-Var 
 <3> Frequency-Watt
 <4> Watt-VAr 
 <5> Voltage-Watt 
 <6> Remain Connected
 <7> Temperature mode
 <8> Pricing signal mode

High Voltage ride-through curves
 <9> HVRT Must Trip 
 <10> HVRT Momentary Cessation 
 
Low Voltage ride-through curves
 <11> LVRT Must Trip 
 <12> LVRT Momentary Cessation 

High Frequency ride-through curves
 <13> HFRT Must Trip 
 <14> HFRT Momentary Cessation

Low Frequency ride-through curves
 <15> LFRT Must Trip 
 <16> LFRT Momentary Cessation

x_units.name:
Independent (X-Value) Units for Curve.  Enumeration:
     <0> Curve is not defined
     <1> Not applicable / Unknown
     <4> Time
     <29> Voltage
     <33> Frequency
     <38> Watts
     <23>  Celsius Temperature
     <100> Price in hundredths of local currency
     <129> Voltage as a percent of nominal voltage (VRef)
     <133> Frequency as a percent of nominal frequency
     <138> Percent Watts
     <233> Frequency Deviation
     <234+> Other

y_units.name:
Dependent (Y-Value) Units for Curve.  Enumeration:
     <0> Curve is not defined
     <1> Not applicable / unknown
     <2> VArs as percent of max VArs (VARMax)
     <3> VArs as percent of max available VArs (VArAval)
     <4> Vars as percent of max apparent Watts (VAMax) - used in IEEE 1547
     <5> Watts as percent of max Watts (Wmax)
     <6> Watts as percent of frozen active power (DeptSnptRef)
     <7> Power Factor in EEI notation
     <8> Volts as a percent of nominal voltage (VRef)
     <9> Frequency as a percent of nominal frequency
    <29> Voltage
    <33> Frequency
    <38> Watts
     <99+>  Other
